articles.title: Pakistan says a new round of peace talks with Afghanistan is underway in China
articles.summary: Pakistan confirmed it was holding peace talks with Afghanistan's Taliban government in China, where Beijing is trying to broker a lasting ceasefire after weeks of fighting.

articles.title: Jobs and Workers Are In Balance. Nobody Is Happy About It.
articles.summary: Lower immigration has brought labor supply in line with shaky demand, but economists worry that such a slow-moving job market is at risk of toppling over.

articles.title: Rival Nations Seize On Choke Points to Counter Trump
articles.summary: From Iran to China, President Trump’s global aggression has encouraged other countries to search for new ways to pressure the U.S. economy.

articles.title: Iran hits Gulf refineries as Trump warns U.S. will attack Iranian bridges, power plants

articles.title: How Pam Bondi Got Fired, and a Flood of Deadly New Drugs
articles.summary: Plus, your Friday news quiz.

articles.title: Verdicts against Meta and Google may bring a new era of big tech accountability
articles.summary: Advocates hope recent verdicts against social media platforms will build momentum for bigger changes in Silicon Valley.

articles.title: At War With Iran Again, Some Israelis Fear Conflict Is Becoming Routine
articles.summary: A majority of Israelis support the war with Iran, but many doubt that it will solve Israel’s long-term security problems. Some also question their prime minister’s assurances and motives.

articles.title: After the release of the Epstein files, why have there been so few arrests?
articles.summary: Legal experts tell NPR five possible reasons that, despite the accusations made against rich and powerful people in the files, the DOJ have made no additional arrests. The big one? Lack of evidence.

articles.title: Evacuation of U.S. troops from Mideast base sends community groups scrambling to help
articles.summary: Troops and their families have been pushed back to the United States after their bases in the Middle East were threatened by Iranian counterattacks. Community groups are scrambling to react.

articles.title: As DOJ prepares to share state voter data with DHS, a key privacy officer resigns
articles.summary: Since last year, the Justice Department has made unprecedented demands to states for sensitive voter data, including drivers license numbers and partial Social Security numbers.

articles.title: NPR wants your big question about reducing your climate impact and saving money
articles.summary: As energy prices rise and climate change intensifies, NPR wants to hear your questions about spending decisions you'll make that could reduce climate pollution and save you money.

articles.title: Penalties stack up as AI spreads through the legal system
articles.summary: Early scandals have not slowed lawyers' adoption of AI tools, even as court sanctions over fake legal briefs continue to rise.

articles.title: Big tech's next move is to put data centers in space. Can it work?
articles.summary: In orbit, power is free. But everything else is expensive.

articles.title: Former U.S. ambassador talks about Trump's rhetoric on NATO
articles.summary: NPR's A Martinez asks Dan Baer, former U.S. ambassador to the Organization for Security and Cooperation in Europe, about the Trump administration's recent rhetoric around NATO.

articles.title: Strait of Hormuz closure deflates global helium supply
articles.summary: The blockade in the Strait of Hormuz is affecting not only oil, but the global helium supply.

articles.title: Cuba releasing 2,010 prisoners as the US pressures the island's government
articles.summary: The Cuban government said the pardons were a "humanitarian gesture" in connection with Holy Week and didn't mention mounting pressures with the U.S.

articles.title: Pam Bondi Wanted a Graceful Exit. But Trump Wanted Her Gone.
articles.summary: Pam Bondi had a feeling her days as attorney general were numbered. But she didn’t expect President Trump to drop the curtain quite so soon.

articles.title: Hegseth Fires Army Chief Amid Battle With Its Leaders
articles.summary: Senior Army officers reacted with anger and frustration to news of Gen. Randy George’s dismissal, characterizing it as the latest blow to the service.

articles.title: What to Know About the ‘Massive’ Military Bunker Beneath Trump’s Ballroom
articles.summary: President Trump has been talking about the emergency facility beneath what was once the East Wing, details of which are usually kept secret, as he tries to justify his renovation.

articles.title: U.K. convenes 40 nations to discuss Strait of Hormuz
articles.summary: Foreign ministers from more than 40 countries are meeting virtually to discuss President Trump's request for help in ensuring shipping in the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: Who Is Todd Blanche, Trump’s Acting Attorney General?
articles.summary: While it remains unclear how long Todd Blanche will stay in the job, whoever ends up taking over permanently will lead a department that he has shaped in his own image.

articles.title: Trump makes case for Iran war. And, SCOTUS leans toward upholding birthright citizenship
articles.summary: President Trump addressed the nation last night, making his case for war with Iran. And, the Supreme Court majority seemed inclined to rule against the Trump administration on birthright citizenship.
